0.q: under the drought and salt treatments, the activities of POD and SOD of the WT and transgenic plants were both significantly increased... the lower ROS accumulation in the OE lines was due to, at least partly, the promoted ROS-scavenging system.

1.q: enhancing the antioxidant enzyme system and maintaining reactive oxygen species (ROS) homeostasis... lower H2O2 content and higher anti-superoxide anion activity were detected in OE lines

2.q: The stomatal apertures of OE1 and OE2 were increased to about 3.2, which was significantly higher than the 2.0 of WT. This result showed that the overexpression of MfWRKY7 facilitated stomatal closure under drought.

3.q: after mannitol treatments, especially with 300 mM concentration, transgenic lines showed a significantly higher degree of stomatal closure than that of WT

4.q: Induced by mannitol, the OE lines showed narrower stomata and higher stomata aperture (ratio of length/width), suggesting that stomata movement of the OE lines responded to mannitol treatment more quickly than the WT did.

5.q: The two OE lines exhibited significantly higher activities of CAT, POD, and SOD under both drought and salt treatments, which were 1.52 and 1.71-fold, 2.05 and 1.71-fold, and 1.31 and 1.56-fold those of WT, respectively.

6.q: the antioxidation ability was also significantly improved... The activities of SOD (superoxide dismutase) and Peroxidase (POD) were measured... transgenic lines overexpressing MfWRKY41 showed better growth performance under drought and salt treatments.

8.q: After treatment by mannitol and ABA, the stomatal apertures of three transgenic lines were reduced to 0.25–0.26 and 0.10–0.12, respectively, which were remarkably lower than those of WT plants.

9.q: Transgenic plants presented apparent stronger activities than those of WT...overexpression of MfPIF1 increased capacities of scavenging ROS under stressful conditions, and hence decreased cellular oxidative damage.

11.q: Treatments of mannitol and ABA reduced stomatal aperture of three transgenic lines to 0.23–0.25 and 0.09–0.10, respectively, which were remarkably lower than those of WT plants (0.32 and 0.21). These results demonstrated that MfbHLH38 promoted stomatal closure in response to mannitol and ABA.

12.q: significantly lower H2O2 content and higher anti-superoxide anion activity were detected in OE lines upon salinity and drought stress compared with WT

13.q: the higher activities of antioxidant enzymes and lower accumulation of O2− and H2O2 in MfWRKY7 transgenic plants were also found, indicating enhanced antioxidation capacity by MfWRKY7.
